prompt: The nightly QC report job has started dying some mornings and I can't see a pattern in WHAT it prints — only in WHEN. It ranks inspection stations by reject rate (worst first, ties alphabetically) and it's been solid for months. Then it crashed the Monday after the paint line sat idle all weekend, ran clean Tuesday through Friday, and crashed again the morning after a station was down for maintenance. Our job wrapper swallows the panic text, so all I have is a nonzero exit and those correlations. When you fix it I need two things guaranteed, and the tests spell both out: (1) a station that inspected zero parts in the window has a reject rate of exactly 0.0 — idle is not a defect — and it sorts with the other zero-rate stations, alphabetical among equals; (2) the sorter must never take the report down again regardless of what rate value reaches it. That second one matters because sort_rows also re-orders rows loaded from old snapshot files, and a few ancient snapshots carry junk rates that print as NaN — those rows must land dead last, alphabetical among themselves, with every normal row keeping its usual order above them. `cargo test --offline` reproduces the crashes and pins the required ordering. Fix src/lib.rs only — tests/, Cargo.toml, and .gitignore stay put.
